name: Kit (ConvertKit) API
what_it_does: Kit (formerly ConvertKit) — creator-focused email marketing platform with a REST API for managing subscribers, tags, sequences, and broadcasts for independent creators and online businesses.
use_cases: Agents managing subscriber lifecycle — adding, tagging, and segmenting email subscribers based on content consumption or purchases
Sequence enrollment — agents automatically enrolling subscribers in email sequences when they complete specific actions
Purchase-based tagging — agents tagging buyers in Kit when Stripe payment events occur for automated follow-up sequences
Broadcast automation — agents creating and scheduling email broadcasts triggered by content publication events
Subscriber analytics — agents querying Kit for open rates, click rates, and subscriber growth for creator analytics
not_for: E-commerce marketing with deep Shopify integration — use Klaviyo for revenue-tracked e-commerce email
Enterprise marketing automation at scale — HubSpot or Marketo for complex enterprise journeys
Transactional email infrastructure — use Postmark or SendGrid for system-generated emails
best_when: Your agent serves independent creators (writers, podcasters, course creators) who need subscriber management, email sequences, and simple automation.
avoid_when: You need complex behavioral automation or e-commerce integration — use Klaviyo or Customer.io.

auth.notes: v3 API uses API Key (legacy). v4 API uses OAuth2 with granular scopes for subscriber_read, subscriber_write, broadcast_write, etc. V4 is preferred for new integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10,000 subscribers, unlimited emails
pricing.paid_tiers: Creator: $9/month (1,000 subscribers, automations); Creator Pro: $25/month (advanced features)
pricing.notes: Generous free tier up to 10K subscribers. Paid plans unlock automations, sequences, and integrations. Creator-focused pricing without per-email charges.

performance.rate_limits: v3 API: 120 req/min; v4 API: varies by scope; webhook delivery: real-time

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented v4 API with OAuth2. Error responses include field-level validation. V3 and V4 have different response formats — agents should standardize on V4.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber creation is idempotent by email — returns existing subscriber. Sequence enrollment is idempotent (re-enrolling is a no-op if already enrolled). Broadcast send is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: v3 and v4 APIs coexist — v3 uses API key, v4 uses OAuth2; response formats differ significantly
Tag names are case-sensitive — 'Customer' and 'customer' are different tags
Sequence enrollment requires knowing sequence ID — agents must discover IDs via API, not names
Email broadcasts cannot be recalled once sent — agents must implement confirmation before sending
Domain verification required for custom domain sending — emails from unverified domains may fail or go to spam

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant. CAN-SPAM compliant. OAuth2 with granular scopes in v4. US data processing. No SOC2 documentation. Established platform (2013) with proven reliability.
